run_notes: Mode 2 run. Previous run (2026-04-08T06-30) patches for ISS-444/462/463 awaiting human application. New patches this run: Cycle 2 WIN-001 improvements (ISS-476, ISS-477, ISS-478). HOL-MISSING issues (ISS-466-475) all assigned to EXP-012 — no wins.json patches needed for those.

patches.find: WIN-001 claims a fundamental resonance of 11.78 Hz while WIN-002 claims 7.83 Hz (with 26% aetheric damping reducing from 10.6 Hz). These are incompatible: if the disc thickness resonance is 11.78 Hz, the Schumann frequency should be near that value, not 7.83 Hz. The model claims credit for two different frequencies from two incompatible calculations.
patches.replace: WIN-001 claims a fundamental resonance of 11.78 Hz while WIN-002 claims 7.83 Hz (with 26% aetheric damping reducing from 10.6 Hz). These are incompatible: if the disc thickness resonance is 11.78 Hz, the Schumann frequency should be near that value, not 7.83 Hz. The model claims credit for two different frequencies from two incompatible calculations. The incompatibility also reveals that aetheric damping is applied selectively: if the 26% damping (WIN-002) were applied consistently, Tesla's 11.78 Hz should reduce to approximately 8.72 Hz. This frequency appears nowhere in the dome model. The 26% figure is applied to Schumann to make it match observation (10.59 Hz → 7.83 Hz) but is not applied to WIN-001's Tesla resonance — demonstrating that the damping constant is a post-hoc fitting parameter, not a physical property of the aether medium.
patches.rationale: Cycle 2 WIN-001 curmudgeon review identified that the current text states incompatibility without showing WHY it proves ad hoc fitting. Adding the selective damping calculation (26% applied to 11.78 Hz should give 8.72 Hz, which never appears in the model) closes the argument and makes it immune to the dome's 'complementary not contradictory' defense.

patches.find: at ~1.57c (a superluminal phase velocity characteristic of the Earth-ionosphere waveguide), not a flat-disc standing-wave resonance.
patches.replace: at ~1.57c (a superluminal phase velocity characteristic of the Earth-ionosphere waveguide — equal to approximately πc/2, the phase velocity of the fundamental TM01 mode of a spherical cavity with Earth's radius), not a flat-disc standing-wave resonance.
patches.rationale: Cycle 2 WIN-001 curmudgeon review (ISS-477): adding the geometric origin (πc/2) for the 1.57c phase velocity clarifies why this superluminal speed is physically unremarkable in a spherical cavity resonator, making it harder for dome defenders to treat the speed as mysterious or non-standard.

patches.find: Furthermore, 11.78 Hz is distinct from the Schumann resonance (7.83 Hz); conflating them misidentifies two separate electromagnetic phenomena.
patches.replace: Furthermore, 11.78 Hz is distinct from the Schumann resonance (7.83 Hz); conflating them misidentifies two separate electromagnetic phenomena. The dome model's own evolution page acknowledges this history: V51.0 states the model "voluntarily removed circular reasoning (Tesla f→T→f)" in an earlier version — an admission that the Tesla-Schumann connection originally required circular derivation. The current version avoids explicit circularity but retains the structural problem: the disc_thickness parameter that produces 11.78 Hz matches Earth's diameter (12,742 km) rather than any dome parameter.
patches.rationale: Cycle 2 WIN-001 curmudgeon review (ISS-478): The dome evolution page (raw-text/06-evolution.txt) explicitly states 'Voluntarily removed circular reasoning (Tesla f->T->f).' Citing this directly strengthens our argument by showing the dome author acknowledged a structural problem in the original Tesla claim. The current version avoids direct circularity but replaces it with globe-parameter relabeling.
